source_jobs.title: Business Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Join us in building the leading AI-Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $4T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.



ABOUT YOU

You are an operator at heart—curious, systematic, and energized by making things run better. You thrive in early-stage environments where processes are still being built and every improvement you make has an immediate impact. You don't just go along for the ride. You act as a driver, not a passenger, spotting inefficiencies and fixing them before anyone has to ask.

You're comfortable moving between people, process, and tooling in the same week. You get satisfaction from a well-designed workflow, a clean CRM, or a hiring process that just works. You want to be part of a small team where your contributions have outsized influence, and where the foundations you build today scale with the business.



ABOUT THE ROLE

 - We are seeking a Business Operations Associate to join our founding team. As one of our first operations hires, you'll help build the internal systems and processes that keep Clarasight running smoothly as we scale.

 - This is not a narrow, task-execution role. You will work across revenue operations, people ops, process design, and automation to make the whole business more efficient and effective.

 - You'll collaborate closely with leadership, sales, and recruiting to identify where things are breaking down and build solutions that last. You'll move quickly, wear multiple hats, and help define the operational foundations that will grow with us through Series A and beyond.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - GTM Operations — Own the systems and workflows that keep Clarasight's go-to-market motion running smoothly. Keep the CRM clean and well-structured, ensure handoffs between marketing and sales work seamlessly, and make sure the team has the tooling and processes it needs to execute effectively.

 - Process Design & Tooling — Identify friction across the business and design better processes. Evaluate, implement, and manage internal tools that improve how teams work, from onboarding to execution.

 - Automation & Optimization — Build automations that eliminate repetitive manual work across sales, hiring, and internal operations. Look for opportunities to do more with less through smart system design.

 - Global Operations — Support hiring coordination, onboarding, and the people processes that make Clarasight a great place to work. Help build the operational infrastructure and cultural foundations that scale as we grow the team across geographies.


YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have 3–5 years of experience in operations, consulting, or a similar role, ideally at a startup or fast-moving company.

 - Are highly organized and take pride in keeping systems clean and processes consistent.

 - Are comfortable working across tools like HubSpot, Notion, Slack, or similar, and quick to learn new ones.

 - Have a builder's mindset and look for ways to automate or improve before accepting the status quo.

 - Can communicate clearly with people across functions and seniority levels.

 - Are energized by variety and thrive when no two weeks look the same.

 - Thrive in fast-paced, early-stage startup environments where the playbook is still being written.


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience working with or administering a CRM (HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar).

 - Familiarity with automation tools like Zapier, Make, or similar.

 - Exposure to recruiting coordination or people operations.

 - Experience at a Series A or early-stage startup.

 - Background in SaaS, travel, or enterprise software.

 - Experience working alongside AI systems and applying human judgment to automated outputs.


DIVERSE PERSPECTIVES

We know that innovation thrives on teams where diverse points of view come to

source_jobs.title: Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Join us in building the leading AI-Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $4T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.


ABOUT YOU

You are a builder at heart — curious, analytical, and energized by turning complex financial and travel data into tools that people actually use. You move fluidly between data science, prototyping, and product thinking, and you're just as comfortable exploring a messy dataset as you are shipping a prototype into customers' hands. You thrive on iteration, customer feedback, and the challenge of making advanced techniques — including machine learning and LLMs — deliver real, measurable value.


ABOUT THE ROLE

 - We are seeking a Data Scientist to create product prototypes that address customer needs and enhance platform capabilities.

 - This role translates analytical insights into actionable tools, using advanced data techniques and customer feedback to shape what ships next.

 - You will work across financial and travel spend data, collaborate closely with advisory, forecasting, and engineering partners, and help define how data science shows up in the Clarasight product.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Prototype & Product Development

 - Design and develop product prototypes based on financial and travel spend analysis and client data.

 - Utilize Python, R, JavaScript, or Tableau to build scalable, testable tools for customers.

 - Iterate on prototypes based on customer feedback to refine and improve usability.

 - Work with engineering teams to operationalize data science workflows, ensuring scalability and long-term viability of prototypes.

Data Integration & Insight Generation

 - Lead data integration efforts across diverse sources and formats, ensuring clean, reliable, and unified datasets that power analytics and product features.

 - Leverage customer datasets to identify trends, generate insights, and create impactful solutions.

 - Collaborate with advisory and forecasting specialists to integrate insights into product design.

Advanced Techniques & Visualization

 - Explore and implement advanced techniques like machine learning, use of large language models for preliminary analyses, or optimization to improve the accuracy, speed, and efficiency of financial analytics tools.

 - Create dynamic visualizations and dashboards to provide clients with actionable financial insights in an intuitive format.


YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have experience in data science with a focus on finance, economics, travel spend analytics, or corporate cost management.

 - Have expertise in data integration — including ETL pipelines, API connections, and harmonizing data across multiple systems and formats.

 - Are proficient in Python, R, or JavaScript for data analysis and tool development.

 - Are familiar with building and testing product prototypes in an iterative environment.

 - Have strong knowledge of corporate finance principles, cost optimization strategies, and economic modeling.

 - Have experience developing data-driven tools and applications that enable stakeholders to make informed financial decisions.

 - Can communicate complex concepts to non-expert audiences effectively.


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience working alongside AI systems and applying human judgment to automated outputs.

 - Hands-on experience with LLMs or ML models applied to financial or spend data.

 - Familiarity with corporate travel, expense, or emissions datasets.

 - Experience shipping prototypes into production alongside engineering teams.


DIVERSE PERSPECTIVES

We know that innovation thrives on teams where diverse points of view come together to solve hard problems in ways that are just now possible. As such, we explicitly seek people that bring diverse life experiences, diverse educational backgroun

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Join us in building the leading AI-Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $4T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.




ABOUT YOU

 - You thrive at the intersection of deep technical craft and high-stakes customer engagement. This isn't a solutions engineering role where you demo software and hand off tickets — you want to be embedded in complex enterprise environments, building real things during live sales cycles and turning what you learn into durable product capabilities.

 - You contribute to production codebases without hesitation, and you've spent meaningful time wrangling messy enterprise data. You communicate clearly across audiences, move fast, and don't need your name on things — you need them to work.

 - You have strong opinions about where AI genuinely creates advantage and where it's noise, and you're energized by turning customer-specific solutions into scalable product.


ABOUT THE ROLE

 - We are seeking a Forward Deployed Engineer to sit within Engineering but spend most of their time in pre-sales and strategic customer engagements, working closely with Sales and Customer Success.

 - This is a build-and-own role: you will lead technical discovery with prospects and customers, architect and prototype integrations and AI workflows during live sales cycles, reconcile messy low-quality enterprise datasets, and contribute directly to Clarasight's full-stack production codebase.

 - Your job is to turn customer-specific solutions into scalable product capabilities — the best things you build shouldn't stay custom.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Customer Engineering

 - Own the technical relationship from first discovery call through contract close.

 - Collaborate with the data science team to translate ambiguous, high-pressure business problems into working prototypes and clear technical narratives that build trust and accelerate deals.

 - Lead technical discovery with prospects and customers.

Data & Systems

 - Design and build pipelines across fragmented travel, financial, and expense systems.

 - Get hands-on with messy, low-quality enterprise data: reconciling, validating, and shaping it into something reliable.

 - Build frameworks that last, not workarounds that don't.

AI Application

 - Apply AI where it genuinely creates advantage: enrichment, anomaly detection, forecasting, automation.

 - Have strong opinions about where it helps and where it's noise.

 - Turn experiments into repeatable, scalable workflows.

Productization

 - Feed structured learnings directly into Product.

 - Turn the best customer-specific solutions into part of the platform.

 - Contribute directly to Clarasight's full-stack production codebase.


YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have 5–8+ years in software engineering, data engineering, or technical consulting.

 - Have a track record of delivering in ambiguous, high-ownership environments.

 - Contribute to production codebases without hesitation.

 - Have spent meaningful time wrangling messy enterprise data.

 - Communicate clearly across technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Move fast and prioritize outcomes over credit.


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience with fintech or enterprise financial systems.

 - Demonstrated outcomes applying AI/ML to real operational data.

 - Prior experience in a forward-deployed, solutions, or founding-engineer capacity at a startup.

 - Exposure to corporate travel, expense, or procurement data.


DIVERSE PERSPECTIVES

Innovation comes from teams where different experiences, backgrounds, and ways of thinking collide. We actively seek people who bring something to the table that we don't already have. Come prepared to tell us what that is.


WE OFFER

 - Generous equity in an early-stage company with serious commercial traction.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Join us in building the leading AI-Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $4T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.



ABOUT YOU

 - You are a builder at heart — curious, pragmatic, and motivated to solve hard technical problems that matter. You thrive in early-stage startup environments where speed, ownership, and adaptability are critical. You don't just go along for the ride — you act as a driver, not a passenger, taking initiative and shaping outcomes rather than waiting for direction.

 - You love working across the stack, from designing intuitive user experiences to architecting robust backend systems, and you're quick to learn new technologies when the problem demands it.

 - You're passionate about leveraging software and data to create meaningful impact, and you see the opportunity to contribute directly to decarbonization and enterprise transformation. You approach challenges with creativity, rigor, and a growth mindset, and you want to be part of a small team where your contributions have outsized influence.


ABOUT THE ROLE

 - We are seeking a Senior Software Engineer to join our founding team. As one of our first engineering hires, you'll help shape both our product and our engineering culture.

 - This is a true full-stack role where you'll build core product features, contribute to data infrastructure, and take ownership of systems from design through deployment.

 - You'll collaborate closely with product and design, experiment rapidly, and help define best practices that will scale with us. You'll be at the center of key technical and strategic decisions as we scale.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Full-Stack Development

 - Design, build, and deploy end-to-end features across frontend, backend, and infrastructure.

 - Ensure code quality, scalability, and maintainability while balancing speed of delivery.

Data Engineering & Systems Design

 - Contribute to building robust data pipelines and integrations that power financial and emissions insights.

 - Collaborate on data modeling, ETL, and analytics foundations to support customer needs.

Startup Agility

 - Wear multiple hats and move quickly to meet customer and product demands.

 - Make pragmatic technical decisions while keeping long-term scalability in mind.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Work closely with Product and Design to deliver features that solve real customer problems.

 - Partner with business teams to understand enterprise requirements and translate them into technical solutions.


YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have 3+ years of professional software engineering experience.

 - Bring full-stack experience (e.g., React/TypeScript, Node.js, Python, or similar).

 - Have startup or early-stage experience and thrive in fast-paced, ambiguous environments.

 - Are excited about automating data engineering processes and already have experience with pipelines, ETL, and analytics systems.

 - Are quick to learn new technologies and approaches.


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience with dbt, DuckDB, or the modern data stack.

 - Familiarity with cloud platforms, DevOps, or infrastructure as code.

 - Background in sustainability, carbon accounting, or financial systems.


DIVERSE PERSPECTIVES

We know that innovation thrives on teams where diverse points of view come together to solve hard problems in ways that are just now possible. As such, we explicitly seek people that bring diverse life experiences, diverse educational backgrounds, diverse cultures, and diverse work experiences. Please be prepared to share with us how your perspective will bring something unique and valuable to our team.

source_jobs.title: Founding Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Join us in building the leading AI-Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $4T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.



ABOUT YOU

 - You are a builder at heart — curious, pragmatic, and motivated by hard infrastructure problems. You thrive in early-stage environments where speed and ownership are critical. You act as a driver, not a passenger, and you want to be part of a small team where your contributions have outsized influence.

 - You love designing systems that scale across clouds, automating everything you can, and building platforms that make other engineers more productive.

 - You care deeply about reliability, security, and operational excellence.


ABOUT THE ROLE

 - We are seeking a Founding Infrastructure Engineer to help build and scale the core platform that powers Clarasight.

 - Our platform runs on AWS as a multi-tenant SaaS product — you'll help extend it to support customer-owned cloud deployments across AWS, GCP, and Azure over time.

 - You'll design deployment automation, build internal tooling, and support enterprise customer deployments, sitting at the intersection of platform reliability, customer delivery, and engineering productivity.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Multi-Cloud Architecture

 - Design infrastructure that runs consistently across cloud providers.

 - Extend our AWS platform to support customer-owned deployments across AWS, GCP, and Azure.

Infrastructure as Code

 - Build and maintain infrastructure with Terraform or similar tools.

 - Develop automated, reproducible deployment pipelines for internal and customer environments.

Customer Deployments

 - Work with enterprise customers to deploy Clarasight into their cloud accounts.

 - Build tooling and documentation that makes deployments scalable and repeatable.

Reliability & Observability

 - Design monitoring, logging, and alerting across distributed environments.

 - Establish best practices for security, networking, and access management.

Engineering Enablement

 - Build internal tooling that makes it easier for product engineers to deploy and operate services.

 - Help define DevOps and CI/CD best practices.


YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have 4+ years in infrastructure, platform engineering, or DevOps.

 - Have strong hands-on AWS experience and familiarity with IaC tools like Terraform.

 - Have built CI/CD pipelines and automated deployment systems.

 - Have designed or operated production systems in cloud environments.

 - Thrive in fast-paced, early-stage startup environments.


NICE TO HAVES

 - Multi-cloud experience (GCP, Azure).

 - Enterprise or customer-managed deployment experience.

 - Kubernetes or container orchestration experience.

 - Multi-tenant SaaS infrastructure background.

 - TypeScript and/or Python development experience.


DIVERSE PERSPECTIVES

We know that innovation thrives on teams where diverse points of view come together to solve hard problems in ways that are just now possible. As such, we explicitly seek people that bring diverse life experiences, diverse educational backgrounds, diverse cultures, and diverse work experiences. Please be prepared to share with us how your perspective will bring something unique and valuable to our team.

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Join us in building the leading AI-Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $4T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.


ABOUT YOU

 - You are passionate about corporate travel, sustainability, and how modern software — especially data- and AI-enabled platforms — can reshape how large organizations make decisions.

 - You thrive in complex, consultative sales environments where problems are not fully defined, stakeholders are diverse, and value must be discovered — not just pitched. You are comfortable engaging senior executives, navigating ambiguity, and building conviction through insight, data, and clear business narratives.

 - You are energized by early-stage, high-growth companies where ownership is real, feedback loops are tight, and your work directly shapes the company's trajectory.


ABOUT THE ROLE

 - We are looking for a strategic sales professional to help drive Clarasight's growth across the enterprise.

 - You will own the full sales cycle for complex, high-impact enterprise opportunities — partnering with senior leaders across travel, procurement, finance, sustainability, and operations to help them reimagine how travel is managed and optimized.

 - You will operate at the intersection of customer strategy, product value, and market insight: shaping deals, influencing product direction, and helping define how Clarasight wins in the enterprise.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Own the Enterprise Sales Cycle

 - Own and drive complex enterprise sales cycles from initial discovery through close and expansion.

 - Engage senior executive stakeholders to uncover strategic priorities, pain points, and success criteria.

 - Translate ambiguous customer needs into clear value propositions, business cases, and solution narratives.

 - Position Clarasight as a trusted, long-term partner — not just a software vendor.

Enterprise Selling & Value Creation

 - Lead discovery conversations that go beyond surface-level requirements to uncover real economic and operational drivers.

 - Develop compelling ROI narratives tied to cost, efficiency, sustainability, and decision quality.

 - Guide customers through change management, stakeholder alignment, and buying complexity.

 - Navigate multi-threaded deals involving procurement, legal, finance, IT, and executive sponsors.

 - Balance urgency and patience in long, strategic sales cycles.

Cross-Functional Partnership & Market Feedback

 - Collaborate closely with Customer Success, Product, and Data teams to ensure smooth handoffs and strong customer outcomes.

 - Build and refine repeatable enterprise sales motions, messaging, and playbooks.

 - Act as a voice of the market, feeding customer insights, objections, and competitive signals back into product and go-to-market strategy.


YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have 7–12+ years of experience in enterprise B2B sales, strategic accounts, or solution-led selling within SaaS, data, or analytics platforms.

 - Have a proven track record selling complex, high-ACV solutions to large enterprises.

 - Are comfortable selling ambiguity — you can lead when the solution is not fully predefined.

 - Have strong business and analytical fluency: you understand how data, metrics, and analytics drive executive decision-making.

 - Can connect product capabilities to real financial and operational outcomes.

 - Thrive in early-stage environments where ownership, judgment, and speed matter.


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience selling into corporate travel, procurement, finance, or sustainability buyers.

 - Background selling AI- or analytics-driven platforms to executive stakeholders.

 - Experience as an early sales hire at a startup, helping build the playbook rather than inherit one.

 - Exposure to deals involving procurement, legal, IT,

source_jobs.title: Solutions Architect
source_jobs.description:
About Us
Join us in building the leading AI Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $5T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.

About You
You are passionate about corporate travel, sustainability, and how modern software, especially AI enabled systems, can drive real world change. You thrive in ambiguous, high impact environments where customer problems are not fully defined, data is imperfect, and speed matters. You are equally comfortable engaging executives, designing solutions, and rolling up your sleeves to build, test, and refine technical workflows directly with customers.

About the Role

 - We are looking for a Solutions Architect to own the design, delivery, and evolution of Clarasight's most complex and high impact customer solutions.

 - You will work hands on with enterprise clients to translate ambiguous travel challenges into concrete, AI enabled solutions that deliver measurable results.

 - You will operate at the boundary between customers, product, and data by rapidly prototyping solutions with clients, shaping scalable delivery patterns, and feeding real world learnings directly into Clarasight's product roadmap.



Key Responsibilities

 - Lead complex enterprise engagements from discovery through measurable value realization.

 - Translate ambiguous customer problems into clear solution architectures, implementation plans, and success metrics.

 - Act as a trusted advisor to leaders across travel, procurement, finance, IT, and sustainability.

 - Rapidly prototype and iterate on solutions directly with customers to prove value early.

 - Serve as a critical field to product feedback loop, shaping roadmap priorities and reusable capabilities.

 - Partner closely with Sales, Product, Data, and Customer Success to drive retention, expansion, and long term impact.

 - Work hands on with customer data, models, configurations, and analytics workflows to build proof of value implementations, dashboards, and planning models.

 - Experiment with AI (LLMs, agents, workflow automation) to accelerate solution delivery and design AI augmented planning and decision support systems.

 - Build custom integrations and workflows that don't yet exist in the product, balancing speed and rigor as requirements, data, and tools evolve.



You'll Be a Great Fit If You…

 - Have 5–8 years in solution architecture, implementation consulting, or enterprise systems design, with a strong track record across solution consulting, forward deployed engineering, or professional services in enterprise data / SaaS.

 - Are comfortable operating as a forward deployed problem solver, not just an advisor.

 - Are technically fluent and data oriented: you understand data models, metrics, transformations, and system workflows.

 - Can query large datasets using SQL or other tools and languages.

 - Can read, shape, and influence technical implementations even if you are not a full time engineer, and can drive hands on configurations and light development work.

 - Are an exceptional communicator with the ability to translate complex technical and analytical concepts into clear executive narratives.

 - Are energized by early stage environments where ownership, judgment, and speed matter.



Nice to Haves

 - AI fluent and experimental by nature. Eager to test tools, refine workflows, and push automation forward.

 - Experience working alongside AI systems and applying human judgment to automated outputs.

 - Prior exposure to corporate travel, procurement, or sustainability domains.

 - Experience building custom integrations or internal tooling in a forward deployed or professional services capacity.



Diverse Perspectives
We know that innovation thrives on teams where diverse points of view come together to solve hard problems in ways tha

source_jobs.title: Founding Sales Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Join us in building the leading AI-Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $4T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.



ABOUT YOU

You are a hands-on technical seller who thrives at the intersection of customer problems, product capability, and revenue. You've been the technical lead on complex enterprise deals and you know what it takes to get them across the line. You're as comfortable in a discovery conversation with a CFO as you are prototyping an integration in Python — and you want to define how a presales function operates, not inherit someone else's playbook.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Clarasight is hiring our first Sales Engineer to help us win the deals that will define the company and category for years to come.

Your primary responsibility is to help us win complex enterprise deals. You will own the technical side of our most important opportunities, turning ambiguous customer problems into clear, compelling solutions that transform prospects into believers — not just buyers.

At the same time, you'll define how we do this at scale: how we run demos, how we prove value, and how customer needs shape the product. If you've ever wanted to be the person who writes the playbook instead of inheriting it, and sees the direct impact on revenue and product, this is that role.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Win enterprise deals (increase win rates and shorten sales cycles)

 - Partner with Sales to lead technical discovery, solution design, demo, and technical deal strategy

 - Deliver high-impact demos and proofs of concept that clearly show business value

 - Act as the technical voice in complex, multi-stakeholder sales cycles

Build how we win

 - Define and refine our demo, proof-of-value, and technical validation approach

 - Create repeatable assets and workflows that improve win rates without slowing us down

 - Establish the foundation of a scalable presales function

Design real solutions

 - Translate customer needs into data models, integrations, and workflows

 - Prototype lightweight solutions (scripts, integrations, AI workflows) where needed

 - Ensure what we sell is grounded in what we can deliver

Shape product and GTM

 - Bring structured, high-signal feedback from deals into Product and Engineering

 - Identify patterns across customers that should be productized

 - Help refine how we position and package the product across use cases

What success looks like in 6–12 months

 - You've played a key role in closing our most important enterprise deals

 - We have a clear, repeatable demo and proof motion that consistently wins

 - Customer requirements are clearly shaping product direction

 - You are the go-to technical partner for high-stakes opportunities


YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have been the technical lead on complex enterprise deals (Solutions Engineer, Sales Engineer, or similar) and know what it takes to get them over the line

 - Are comfortable owning outcomes — taking responsibility for whether deals move forward, not just your part in the process

 - Can run discovery, design solutions, and deliver demos that directly influence buying decisions

 - Are hands-on: comfortable writing code (Python, JavaScript, or similar) to prototype integrations, manipulate data, or validate workflows

 - Operate well in ambiguity and can move quickly with imperfect information

 - Think in systems — you don't just solve one deal, you build ways to solve many

 - Communicate clearly and confidently with both technical teams and senior business stakeholders


NICE TO HAVES

 - Knowledge of the tech ecosystem we typically encounter (e.g. TMC, OBT, Expense, Card)

 - Experience working alongside AI systems and applying human judgment to automated outputs


DIVERSE PERSPECTIVES

We know that innovation thri

source_jobs.title: Founding Revenue Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Join us in building the leading AI-Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $4T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.



ABOUT YOU

You are a systems thinker who gets excited by clean data, well-designed processes, and tools that actually talk to each other. You've spent enough time in RevOps to know what "good" looks like, but you're still hands-on enough to build it yourself. You're equally comfortable building a forecast model in a spreadsheet, debugging a broken Salesforce integration, and walking a sales leader through a comp plan redesign. You thrive in environments where you own the entire stack and the buck stops with you.



ABOUT THE ROLE

As the founding Revenue Operations hire at Clarasight, you'll architect the systems, processes, and data infrastructure that power our go-to-market engine. This is a true 0-to-1 role: you'll own Salesforce, the full GTM tech stack, our forecasting cadence, and compensation design from day one. You'll be the connective tissue between Sales, CS, Finance, and Leadership — ensuring every revenue decision is grounded in clean data and defensible logic. The decisions you make and the foundation you lay will shape how we scale revenue for years to come.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Sales Forecasting & Reporting

 - Build and maintain a reliable forecast model - bottoms-up pipeline to board-level view — sourced from clean Salesforce data.

 - Own the weekly forecast cadence with the sales team; coach reps on deal hygiene that feeds accurate data upstream.

 - Design and publish a GTM metrics framework: pipeline coverage, conversion rates by stage, velocity, win/loss analysis, and rep productivity.

 - Leverage data from across the stack (Apollo sequences, Grain call insights, Sales Navigator activity) to enrich pipeline analysis and identify patterns.

 - Partner with Finance to align revenue reporting and ensure a single source of truth across systems.

Hubspot & AI GTM Tech Stack Ownership
Hubspot is the foundation, but this is a full-stack ownership role. You'll be accountable for every tool in the revenue team's arsenal and the integrations that tie them together.

 - Partner with our AI Automation Engineer to integrate the entire GTM stack, ultimately delivering speed, accuracy, and insights to Leadership and the Sales team.

 - Own Hubspot end-to-end: data model, object architecture, workflow automation, permissions, and hygiene. You build it, you maintain it, you improve it.

 - Manage and optimize the full GTM tech stack including own every integration between tools

 - Maintain a living map of the tech stack: what each tool does, who owns it, what it costs, and how it connects. Run a regular audit to eliminate redundancy and identify gaps.

 - Lead all tool procurement, renewals, and vendor relationships - evaluate new tools with a skeptic's eye and a clear ROI framework.

 - Build and enforce data governance standards across the stack: field naming conventions, lead routing logic, deduplication rules, and enrichment workflows.

 - Drive adoption across the revenue team; build the training, documentation, and change management so tools actually get used the way they were designed.

Compensation & Quota Planning

 - Design and administer Sales and CS commission plans that incentivize the right behaviors at this stage of the company.

 - Run the annual and mid-year quota-setting process, grounded in market data, attainment history, and pipeline capacity models.

 - Build the infrastructure to calculate, verify, and communicate commissions accurately and on time - ideally sourced directly from Salesforce data.


YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have 4–8 years of RevOps, Sales Ops, or GTM Ops experience — enough to have seen what good looks like, not so much that you've for

source_jobs.title: Founding Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Clarasight is the leading AI-powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies — representing over $5B in managed travel spend — rely on us to turn travel spend into a strategic advantage. We are growing fast, landing marquee logos, and building the GTM engine that will define the next chapter.

About You

You are genuinely curious about how businesses work — how companies operate, how executives think, and how travel fits into that picture. You communicate with clarity and confidence, equally at home on the phone with a CFO or writing a cold email. You are resilient, able to hear no all day, stay positive, and come back sharper tomorrow. You think in systems, not one-offs: you don't just want to book one meeting, you want to figure out how to book a hundred. You treat AI tools as a default, not a novelty.

About the Role

This is not a traditional SDR role. You will be the first SDR at Clarasight, working directly with our co-founders to scale the outbound motion. You will be trained on MEDDPICC from day one, and top performers have a clear path to enterprise Account Executive within 12 months. No politics. No waiting list.

Key Responsibilities

Outbound & Pipeline Generation

 - Identify and engage senior decision-makers (Finance, Procurement, HR, Operations) at large enterprise accounts through cold outreach across phone, email, LinkedIn, and events

 - Multi-thread into accounts and expand coverage within existing opportunities

Strategic Selling

 - Develop exec-level insight into how travel intersects with cost, risk, and business performance — driving strategic conversations, not just booking meetings

Building the Motion

 - Help define what modern outbound looks like by testing approaches across channels, creating repeatable frameworks, and reporting back what is landing and what is not

 - Leverage AI tools by default to research accounts, personalize outreach at scale, and sharpen targeting

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Work closely with Sales and Marketing to refine targeting, messaging, and sequencing

You'll Be a Great Fit If You…

 - You are genuinely curious about business: how companies work, how executives think, and how travel fits into that picture

 - You communicate with clarity and confidence, whether on the phone with a CFO or writing a cold email

 - You are resilient: you can hear no all day, stay positive, and come back sharper tomorrow

 - You think in systems: you don't just want to book one meeting, you want to figure out how to book a hundred

 - You are comfortable with AI tools and expect to use them as a default, not a novelty

Nice to Haves

 - Exposure to enterprise software sales or B2B GTM

 - Familiarity with the corporate travel ecosystem (TMC, OBT, expense, card)

 - Experience building something, even outside of work

 - Experience working alongside AI systems and applying human judgment to automated outputs.

Diverse Perspectives

We know that innovation thrives on teams where diverse points of view come together to solve hard problems in ways that are just now possible. As such, we explicitly seek people that bring diverse life experiences, diverse educational backgrounds, diverse cultures, and diverse work experiences. Please be prepared to share with us how your perspective will bring something unique and valuable to our team.

source_jobs.title: Founding Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Join us in building the leading AI-Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $4T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.

About You

You are a builder at heart — an empty org chart and an unwritten playbook are the best part of the job, not the scary part. You have run full-cycle searches end to end and know how to turn a hiring manager's rough wish list into a signed offer. You are equal parts relationship-driven and operationally rigorous: you can win over a passive candidate and just as easily build out an ATS, define a scorecard, and report on funnel health. You are excited by the idea of a recruiting function that runs on automation and AI rather than manual busywork. You care deeply about candidate experience because every interaction is the company's first impression. You thrive in ambiguity — when there's no process you create one, and when it breaks you fix it without being asked.

About the Role

As Clarasight's Founding Recruiter, you'll own talent acquisition for the whole company at the moment it matters most. This is our first dedicated recruiting hire — you'll partner directly with our founders and hiring managers to build the team that builds the product, recruiting across engineering, go-to-market, and operations while designing the systems and hiring bar that scale with us across Boston, NYC, Lisbon, and London. As an AI company, we expect our recruiting function to reflect it — you'll bring automation and AI into the process wherever it raises speed, signal, or candidate experience.

 - Own full-cycle recruiting across every function — sourcing, screening, interview coordination, and close

 - Manage and build out Ashby as our system of record: pipelines, structured interview loops, scorecards, and hiring metrics

 - Bring automation and AI into the recruiting workflow to increase speed and signal without sacrificing candidate experience

 - Partner with founders and hiring managers to scope roles, calibrate on bar, and run high-signal processes

 - Shape and champion Clarasight's employer brand to attract exceptional talent in a competitive market

Key Responsibilities

 - Source and engage top active and passive candidates across technical and GTM roles

 - Run structured, consistent interview processes and keep every search moving with urgency

 - Own and optimize Ashby — workflows, automations, and integrations — as the backbone of how we hire

 - Establish and own hiring data — pipeline health, conversion, time-to-fill — and use it to improve

 - Identify and deploy automation and AI tooling to streamline sourcing, screening, and coordination

 - Manage agency and tooling relationships and decide when to build in-house vs. outsource

 - Deliver a high-touch candidate experience from first outreach through offer

 - Lay the foundation for a future talent team: playbooks, process, and a culture of hiring well

You'll Be a Great Fit If You…

 - Have 5+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience, ideally in-house at a high-growth startup

 - Have recruited across multiple functions (engineering + GTM a strong plus)

 - Have built or meaningfully improved recruiting processes, not just executed within them

 - Are fluent in modern recruiting tooling and eager to apply automation and AI to the work

 - Are an exceptional communicator who can represent the company and close candidates

 - Operate with high agency and real comfort in ambiguity

 - Are genuinely excited about startup life and building from zero

Nice to Haves

 - Hands-on experience administering and building in Ashby

 - Experience hiring across multiple geographies, including Europe (Lisbon / London)

 - A track record of technical or engineering recruiting in a competitive market

 - Experience working alongside AI systems and apply

source_jobs.title: Founding Product Builder
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Join us in building the leading AI-Powered Mission Control for Corporate Travel. Some of the world's most recognizable companies, representing over $4T in market value, rely on Clarasight's software to reduce risks, save costs, protect revenues, and manage emissions aligned with business objectives and sustainability goals.

About You

You think like a designer first and a product person close behind. You've been the design lead on a lean team with no thick PM layer — the one who talked to customers directly, framed the problem, designed the answer, and drove it into engineering. You set your own pace and don't wait for a spec to land on your desk. You care about craft and about the customer problem in equal measure, and you're energized by the chance to define something from the ground up rather than maintain what someone else built.

About the Role

This is our first dedicated Product Builder hire — and it's a high-ownership one. You'll own design craft and customer-facing problem-solving end to end, partnering directly with our Head of Product and a ~15-person engineering team. You'll establish the design system that brings our platform together, rebuild key flows as we modernize the product, and run your own discovery and validation with customers rather than waiting for direction. The role sits deliberately at the intersection of product design and product management: you'll flex across both, using strong design craft and sharp product instincts to find problems and fix them. As the team grows, there's a real path to specialize more deeply in either direction based on where your strengths take you.

Key Responsibilities

Design System & Consistency

 - Establish our design system. Build the foundational system in Figma — tokens, components, patterns, and usage guidelines — and roll it out feature by feature as we rebuild flows. You'll define it and partner with engineering to make sure it lives consistently in code.

 - Bring consistency to the platform. Create a cohesive look and feel across modules and features — color, typography, components, interaction patterns — so the product feels like one product.

Feature Design & Prototyping

 - Design and prototype key features. Take features from concept to high-fidelity, interactive prototypes, moving fast in Figma and with AI-assisted design tools to get to something real and testable. We don't expect you to write production code.

 - Own discovery on solution paths. For the features you take on, run the solution discovery — understand the problem, explore directions, and make the call on the best path forward.

 - Translate solutions into clear PRDs. Work with the Head of Product to build documents engineering can build from.

Customer Validation & Delivery

 - Validate with customers. Meet with customers directly to understand needs and pressure-test your thinking, and run UI/UX validation on releases — put designs in front of customers, gather feedback, and iterate.

 - Drive tactical delivery. Partner with engineering to ship, moving between focused areas of the product as priorities shift.

You'll Be a Great Fit If You…

 - Have 5+ years of product design experience, ideally in B2B or SaaS, with a portfolio that shows strong visual and interaction craft.

 - Have established a design system — not just contributed to one — and know how to build the foundations and roll them out pragmatically on a real product.

 - Are comfortable owning the problem, not just the pixels: running your own discovery, talking to customers, and making product judgment calls when the direction isn't fully defined.

 - Can write clear requirements and partner closely with engineering to get work shipped.

 - Work fluently in Figma and Claude Design, and reach for AI-assisted design tools to prototype quickly.

 - Thrive in a lean, fast-moving environment where you set the pace and aren't waiting to be handed tickets.

Nice to Haves

 - Experience designing for complex or technical pl
